items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Henry Hub Reclaims $3 Even as Warm Weather Weighs
items.summary: Physical natural gas prices were mixed Friday as balmy spring weather threatened to crush near-term heating demand across much of the country. Gains were concentrated across the middle of the country, including benchmark Henry Hub, which climbed back

items.title: Futures Settle: Natural Gas Gains as War Risks Counter Soft Spring Demand
items.summary: Natural gas futures climbed Friday as escalating Middle East tensions and global energy price jumps offset weakening U.S. spring fundamentals.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Henry Hub Cash Climbs, Waha Sinks Amid Mixed Fundamentals
items.summary: Mixed pricing trends in the natural gas spot market reflected a mild domestic demand outlook and ripple effects of turmoil in the Middle East heading into the weekend.

items.title: Cedar LNG Build Moves Ahead While Gas Supply Questions Loom Over Future Expansion
items.summary: Construction progress of the Cedar LNG project and relaxing constraints in Western Canada is lifting outlooks for oilsands producers, according to Pembina Pipeline Corp. management, but future expansions still hinge on whether the basin can provide m

items.title: Why U.S. Natural Gas Prices Follow Oil Higher Amid Escalating Iran Tensions
items.summary: Lower 48 natural gas futures rallied in the wake of a surge in oil prices amid the U.S.-Israeli military strikes on Iran, highlighting how geopolitical shocks can align energy markets that often move independently.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Rallying as Iran War Impacts Reverberate
items.summary: Natural gas futures appeared headed for impressive weekly gains after an eventful six days in the energy markets as U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran disrupted global supply of LNG and oil.

items.title: U.S. LNG Dominance ‘Fearsome,’ as Energy Tech Development ‘Miles Ahead of Anyone Else,’ Souki Says
items.summary: The United States is no longer merely a market participant but the fundamental anchor of the global energy landscape, according to LNG industry pioneer Charif Souki.

items.title: Risk of Lengthy Mideast War Pushes U.S. Natural Gas Forward Prices Higher
items.summary: An open-ended conflict in the Middle East that has spread to a dozen countries and stifled energy production there has pushed U.S. natural gas prices for this summer and even next winter higher as the market weighs how strong LNG demand could impact

items.title: Trump Shrugs Off Biggest Gas Price Spike in Years
items.summary: Americans are experiencing a sharp rise in gas prices this week, with the national average gasoline price posting its largest weekly jump since the early days of the Russia-Ukraine war. If fuel prices continue to climb as the U.S.-Israeli Operation E

items.title: US Drillers Add Oil Rigs as WTI Jumps 14%
items.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 551 this week, down 41 from this same time last year.

items.title: Reliance Industries Pivots Back to Russian Oil with U.S. Waiver

items.title: U.S. Gasoline Surges to Highest Under Trump as Iran War Roils Oil Market

items.title: Kuwait Shuts Production, Qatar Warns Oil Could Hit $150 in Weeks
items.summary: Oil prices could soar to as much as $150 per barrel within two to three weeks if the critical Strait of Hormuz remains off limits for tankers, Qatar’s Energy Minister Saad al-Kaabi told the Financial Times in an interview published on Friday.   That

items.title: Europe’s Gas Price Set for Largest Weekly Gain in Three Years
